name: Enterprise / line-of-business system
summary: An internal business system (ERP/CRM/workflow/case-management) that automates structured, role-based business processes over a rich, long-lived data model, integrated with the enterprise's identity, legacy systems, and reporting estate. Correctness, auditability, and process fit dominate over aesthetics.

concerns: multi-step, role-based workflows with approvals, delegation, escalation, and SLA timers
state-machine correctness: valid transitions, guards, reopen/rollback, and exception paths
business-rule engine and configurable policy (per-org, per-region variation)
completeness across the long tail of edge cases, overrides, and manual interventions real operations require
bulk operations, batch jobs, and scheduled processing alongside interactive use
questions: Are all workflow states, transitions, guards, and exception/rollback paths specified, not just the happy path?
Are approval, delegation, and escalation rules (including SLA timers) defined?
Are configurable business rules and their per-org/region variation captured?

concerns: complex normalized relational model with referential integrity and transactional consistency
master-data management, golden records, deduplication, and ownership/stewardship
reference/lookup data, effective-dated records, and temporal/bitemporal history
data migration from legacy systems: mapping, cleansing, reconciliation, and cutover validation
reporting/analytics data model, extracts, and read-model separation from the transactional store
data quality dimensions (accuracy, completeness, consistency) with validation at entry
questions: Is the canonical data model, with referential-integrity and transactional-consistency rules, specified?
Is master-data ownership, deduplication, and effective-dating defined?
Is the legacy-migration mapping, cleansing, and reconciliation approach specified with cutover validation?

concerns: fine-grained RBAC/ABAC: roles, entitlements, field-level and record-level (row) security
segregation of duties (SoD) to prevent toxic permission combinations (e.g. create + approve)
enterprise identity integration: SSO via SAML/OIDC, SCIM provisioning, directory group mapping
privileged-access management, break-glass/emergency access with justification and review
least-privilege on integrations and service accounts; secrets management
questions: Is the RBAC/ABAC model specified down to record/field-level where the domain requires it?
Are segregation-of-duties constraints defined to block toxic permission combinations?
Is SSO/SCIM directory integration and de-provisioning on leaver events specified?

concerns: auditability for financial/regulatory compliance (SOX, internal controls): who did what, when, before/after values, immutable and tamper-evident
records-retention schedules and legal hold with defensible deletion
GDPR/sector-specific data-subject rights over long-lived business records
controls evidence and access-recertification / periodic entitlement review
data residency for multinational deployments
questions: Is a tamper-evident audit trail capturing actor, action, timestamp, and before/after values required for compliance?
Are records-retention schedules and legal-hold/defensible-deletion rules specified?
Is periodic access-recertification of entitlements required?

concerns: integration with legacy and adjacent systems (mainframe, ERP, middleware) via APIs, files, message queues, ESB/iPaaS
canonical exchange formats and versioned contracts; EDI/industry schemas where applicable
batch file interfaces (SFTP, fixed-width/CSV) alongside real-time APIs
idempotent, ordered, and replayable integration with reconciliation between systems of record
backward-compatible API evolution as downstream consumers lag
questions: Are the legacy/adjacent-system interfaces, protocols, and versioned contracts specified?
Are batch/file and real-time integration paths defined with reconciliation between systems of record?
Is integration idempotent, ordered, and replayable to survive partner outages?

concerns: configurability without code change (workflows, rules, forms, reports) for evolving business policy
long service life: multi-year support, upgrade paths, and knowledge continuity
modular boundaries so ERP/CRM modules and integrations evolve independently
testability of complex business rules; regression coverage for configuration changes
observability into workflow/job state for support teams
questions: Can workflows, rules, and forms be changed by configuration rather than code, and is that scope specified?
Are automated regression tests for business rules and configuration changes required?
Is workflow/job-state observability for support defined?

concerns: fit to existing organizational structure, roles, and approval hierarchies (Conway's law risk)
change management and adoption: training, phased rollout, resistance from displaced manual processes
cross-departmental governance and ownership of shared data/processes
organizational policies and internal-control frameworks the system must enforce
geopolitical / cross-border constraints for multinational operations
questions: Does the system fit the existing organizational hierarchy, roles, and approval chains?
Are change-management, training, and phased-adoption requirements captured?
Are the organizational policies and internal controls the system must enforce specified?

concerns: transactional integrity across multi-entity updates and long-running workflows (sagas, compensations)
availability aligned to business-day/close-cycle windows; batch-window guarantees
backup/restore, RTO/RPO for systems of record; point-in-time recovery
recoverability of in-flight workflows after failure without duplicate side effects
data reconciliation and integrity checks after failures
questions: Is transactional integrity across multi-entity and long-running workflows specified with compensations?
Are availability targets aligned to business/close-cycle and batch windows?
Can in-flight workflows recover after failure without duplicate side effects?

concerns: high-throughput data-entry ergonomics for expert power users (keyboard-driven, dense grids, bulk edit)
error prevention and clear recovery on complex forms and irreversible business actions
role-tailored views that expose only relevant tasks/fields
discoverability of infrequent but critical operations across a large feature surface
questions: Are data-entry ergonomics for expert power users (keyboard, dense grids, bulk edit) specified?
Are safeguards and clear recovery required for irreversible business actions?

concerns: scheduled-job and batch orchestration with monitoring, alerting, and rerun on failure
environment management (dev/test/UAT/prod) and configuration promotion
deployment during maintenance windows with rollback; on-prem/hybrid hosting realities
operational reporting and admin tooling for support/help-desk tiers
questions: Are scheduled/batch jobs monitored with alerting and defined rerun-on-failure behaviour?
Are environment management and configuration-promotion requirements specified?
Is the hosting model (on-prem/hybrid/cloud) and its deployment/rollback approach defined?
